description:
sr9800: Add check for usbnet_get_endpoints

Add check for usbnet_get_endpoints() and return the error if it fails
in order to transfer the error.
predicate: The return value must be checked for errors and propagated if the function fails.
generalized_target: function that may fail during initialization
generalized_predicate: The return value must be checked for errors and properly propagated to the caller when the function fails.

description:
powerpc/iommu: fix memory leak with using debugfs_lookup()

When calling debugfs_lookup() the result must have dput() called on it,
otherwise the memory will leak over time.  To make things simpler, just
call debugfs_lookup_and_remove() instead which handles all of the logic
at once.
target: A call to function debugfs_lookup that returns a dentry pointer
predicate: The returned dentry must have dput() called on it to prevent memory leak, or debugfs_lookup_and_remove() should be used instead
generalized_target: function that Return a reference requiring explicit cleanup
generalized_predicate: The returned reference must have its corresponding cleanup function called to prevent resource leak

description:
ipmi:ipmb: Fix refcount leak in ipmi_ipmb_probe

of_parse_phandle() returns a node pointer with refcount
incremented, we should use of_node_put() on it when done.
Add missing of_node_put() to avoid refcount leak.
target: A call to function of_parse_phandle that returns a device node pointer with incremented refcount.
predicate: The returned device node pointer must be released with of_node_put() when done to avoid refcount leak.
generalized_target: function that returns an object with incremented reference count
generalized_predicate: The returned object must be released with the corresponding decrement function when no longer needed to avoid reference count leak

description:
net: dsa: bcm_sf2: Fix a possible memory leak in bcm_sf2_mdio_register()

bcm_sf2_mdio_register() calls of_phy_find_device() and then
phy_device_remove() in a loop to remove existing PHY devices.
of_phy_find_device() eventually calls bus_find_device(), which calls
get_device() on the returned struct device * to increment the refcount.
The current implementation does not decrement the refcount, which causes
memory leak.

This commit adds the missing phy_device_free() call to decrement the
refcount via put_device() to balance the refcount.
target: A call to of_phy_find_device() followed by phy_device_remove().
predicate: The returned phy_device must be freed with phy_device_free() to balance the refcount.
generalized_target: Function that acquires a reference-counted device object
generalized_predicate: When the device object is no longer needed, its reference count must be decremented by calling the appropriate release function

description:
ALSA: memalloc: prefer dma_mapping_error() over explicit address checking

With CONFIG_DMA_API_DEBUG enabled, the following warning is observed:

DMA-API: snd_hda_intel 0000:03:00.1: device driver failed to check map error[device address=0x00000000ffff0000] [size=20480 bytes] [mapped as single]
WARNING: CPU: 28 PID: 2255 at kernel/dma/debug.c:1036 check_unmap+0x1408/0x2430
CPU: 28 UID: 42 PID: 2255 Comm: wireplumber Tainted: G  W L  6.12.0-10-133577cad6bf48e5a7848c4338124081393bfe8a+ #759
debug_dma_unmap_page+0xe9/0xf0
snd_dma_wc_free+0x85/0x130 [snd_pcm]
snd_pcm_lib_free_pages+0x1e3/0x440 [snd_pcm]
snd_pcm_common_ioctl+0x1c9a/0x2960 [snd_pcm]
snd_pcm_ioctl+0x6a/0xc0 [snd_pcm]
...

Check for returned DMA addresses using specialized dma_mapping_error()
helper which is generally recommended for this purpose by
Documentation/core-api/dma-api.rst.
target: A call to dma_map_single for DMA memory mapping
predicate: The returned DMA address must be checked using dma_mapping_error before being used.
generalized_target: function that performs DMA memory mapping
generalized_predicate: The returned DMA address must be checked using dma_mapping_error before being used or processed further.

description:
ata: pata_octeon_cf: Fix refcount leak in octeon_cf_probe

of_find_device_by_node() takes reference, we should use put_device()
to release it when not need anymore.
Add missing put_device() to avoid refcount leak.

generalized_target: Function that acquires a reference to a device object
generalized_predicate: The reference must be properly released with put_device() when the device is no longer needed, before all possible exit paths from the function.

description:
PCI: Fix reset_method_store() memory leak

In reset_method_store(), a string is allocated via kstrndup() and assigned
to the local "options". options is then used in with strsep() to find
spaces:

  while ((name = strsep(&options, " ")) != NULL) {

If there are no remaining spaces, then options is set to NULL by strsep(),
so the subsequent kfree(options) doesn't free the memory allocated via
kstrndup().

Fix by using a separate tmp_options to iterate with strsep() so options is
preserved.
target: A string allocated via kstrndup and processed with strsep.
predicate: The original allocated string must be preserved and freed, not the modified pointer from strsep.
generalized_target: function that processes an allocated string with a destructive parsing operation
generalized_predicate: The original allocated string pointer must be preserved and freed, not the modified pointer from the parsing operation.
target_descriptions: {"get_options": "char * get_options ( const char * str , int nints , int * ints ) Parse a string into a list of integers", "kstrndup": "char * kstrndup ( const char * s , size_t max , gfp_t gfp ) allocate space for and copy an existing string"}

description:
thermal: intel: int340x: processor: Fix warning during module unload

The processor_thermal driver uses pcim_device_enable() to enable a PCI
device, which means the device will be automatically disabled on driver
detach.  Thus there is no need to call pci_disable_device() again on it.

With recent PCI device resource management improvements, e.g. commit
f748a07a0b64 ("PCI: Remove legacy pcim_release()"), this problem is
exposed and triggers the warining below.

 [  224.010735] proc_thermal_pci 0000:00:04.0: disabling already-disabled device
 [  224.010747] WARNING: CPU: 8 PID: 4442 at drivers/pci/pci.c:2250 pci_disable_device+0xe5/0x100
 ...
 [  224.010844] Call Trace:
 [  224.010845]  <TASK>
 [  224.010847]  ? show_regs+0x6d/0x80
 [  224.010851]  ? __warn+0x8c/0x140
 [  224.010854]  ? pci_disable_device+0xe5/0x100
 [  224.010856]  ? report_bug+0x1c9/0x1e0
 [  224.010859]  ? handle_bug+0x46/0x80
 [  224.010862]  ? exc_invalid_op+0x1d/0x80
 [  224.010863]  ? asm_exc_invalid_op+0x1f/0x30
 [  224.010867]  ? pci_disable_device+0xe5/0x100
 [  224.010869]  ? pci_disable_device+0xe5/0x100
 [  224.010871]  ? kfree+0x21a/0x2b0
 [  224.010873]  pcim_disable_device+0x20/0x30
 [  224.010875]  devm_action_release+0x16/0x20
 [  224.010878]  release_nodes+0x47/0xc0
 [  224.010880]  devres_release_all+0x9f/0xe0
 [  224.010883]  device_unbind_cleanup+0x12/0x80
 [  224.010885]  device_release_driver_internal+0x1ca/0x210
 [  224.010887]  driver_detach+0x4e/0xa0
 [  224.010889]  bus_remove_driver+0x6f/0xf0
 [  224.010890]  driver_unregister+0x35/0x60
 [  224.010892]  pci_unregister_driver+0x44/0x90
 [  224.010894]  proc_thermal_pci_driver_exit+0x14/0x5f0 [processor_thermal_device_pci]
 ...
 [  224.010921] ---[ end trace 0000000000000000 ]---

Remove the excess pci_disable_device() calls.

[ rjw: Subject and changelog edits ]
target: A call to pcim_enable_device() for enabling a PCI device
predicate: The PCI device must not be disabled with pci_disable_device() if pcim_enable_device() was used for enabling it.
generalized_target: Function that enables a device using managed resources
generalized_predicate: The device must not be manually disabled if it was enabled using managed resource functions

description:
bpf, verifier: Fix memory leak in array reallocation for stack state

If an error (NULL) is returned by krealloc(), callers of realloc_array()
were setting their allocation pointers to NULL, but on error krealloc()
does not touch the original allocation. This would result in a memory
resource leak. Instead, free the old allocation on the error handling
path.

The memory leak information is as follows as also reported by Zhengchao:

  unreferenced object 0xffff888019801800 (size 256):
  comm "bpf_repo", pid 6490, jiffies 4294959200 (age 17.170s)
  hex dump (first 32 bytes):
    00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00  ................
    00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00  ................
  backtrace:
    [<00000000b211474b>] __kmalloc_node_track_caller+0x45/0xc0
    [<0000000086712a0b>] krealloc+0x83/0xd0
    [<00000000139aab02>] realloc_array+0x82/0xe2
    [<00000000b1ca41d1>] grow_stack_state+0xfb/0x186
    [<00000000cd6f36d2>] check_mem_access.cold+0x141/0x1341
    [<0000000081780455>] do_check_common+0x5358/0xb350
    [<0000000015f6b091>] bpf_check.cold+0xc3/0x29d
    [<000000002973c690>] bpf_prog_load+0x13db/0x2240
    [<00000000028d1644>] __sys_bpf+0x1605/0x4ce0
    [<00000000053f29bd>] __x64_sys_bpf+0x75/0xb0
    [<0000000056fedaf5>] do_syscall_64+0x35/0x80
    [<000000002bd58261>] entry_SYSCALL_64_after_hwframe+0x63/0xcd
target: A call to krealloc_array for memory reallocation.
predicate: The original allocation must be freed with kfree if krealloc_array fails.
generalized_predicate: If the reallocation fails, the original allocation must be freed before returning.

description:
remoteproc: Fix count check in rproc_coredump_write()

Check count for 0, to avoid a potential underflow. Make the check the
same as the one in rproc_recovery_write().

description:
phy: qcom-qmp: fix struct clk leak on probe errors

Make sure to release the pipe clock reference in case of a late probe
error (e.g. probe deferral).
target: A call to of_clk_get_by_name that acquires a clock reference
predicate: The clock reference must be automatically released on probe errors by using devm_get_clk_from_child instead of of_clk_get_by_name
generalized_target: function that Acquires a resource that requires manual cleanup in probe functions
generalized_predicate: The resource acquisition should use device-managed variants to ensure automatic cleanup on probe failure

description:
ALSA: hda: intel-sdw-acpi: fix usage of device_get_named_child_node()

The documentation for device_get_named_child_node() mentions this
important point:

"
The caller is responsible for calling fwnode_handle_put() on the
returned fwnode pointer.
"

Add fwnode_handle_put() to avoid a leaked reference.

Message-ID: <20240426152731.38420-1-pierre-louis.bossart@linux.intel.com>
predicate: The returned fwnode handle must be released with fwnode_handle_put().
generalized_target: Function that acquires a reference to a firmware node
generalized_predicate: The acquired reference must be released with the corresponding put function when no longer needed.
